        gpu_rem_mem = torch.full((gpu_num,), GPU_MEM_SIZE, dtype=torch.float32)
        gpu_rem_pressure = torch.full((gpu_num,), limit_pressure, dtype=torch.float32)
        
        for idx in sorted_indices:
            s = sizes[idx].item()
            p = pressures[idx].item()
            
            # Find feasible GPUs
            feasible_mask = (gpu_rem_mem >= s) & (gpu_rem_pressure >= p)
            if not feasible_mask.any():
                return False
            
            # Best fit: minimize remaining memory * pressure
            rem_mem = gpu_rem_mem - s
            rem_pressure = gpu_rem_pressure - p
            scores = rem_mem * rem_pressure
            
            # Mask infeasible
            scores[~feasible_mask] = float('inf')
            
            best_gpu = torch.argmin(scores).item()
            
            gpu_rem_mem[best_gpu] -= s
            gpu_rem_pressure[best_gpu] -= p
            
        return True

    # Binary Search
    low = lower_bound
    high = total_w / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - total_s) if total_s < GPU_MEM_SIZE else 1000.0
    
    # Adjust high if total size is very close to total capacity
    if total_s > gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE * 0.9:
        high *= 1.3
    
    # Run binary search with sufficient precision
    for _ in range(60):
        mid = (low + high) / 2
        if is_feasible(mid):
            high = mid
        else:
            low = mid
    
    # --- Final Assignment Construction ---
    final_limit = high
    limit_pressure = final_limit * GPU_MEM_SIZE
    pressures = weights + final_limit * sizes
    
    # Sort by pressure descending
    sorted_indices = torch.argsort(pressures, descending=True)
    
    # Initialize GPU resources
    gpu_rem_mem = torch.full((gpu_num,), GPU_MEM_SIZE, dtype=torch.float32)
    gpu_rem_pressure = torch.full((gpu_num,), limit_pressure, dtype=torch.float32)
    assignment_indices = [[] for _ in range(gpu_num)]
    
    # Assign models greedily with best-fit heuristic
    for idx in sorted_indices:
        s = sizes[idx].item()
        p = pressures[idx].item()
        
        # Check feasible GPUs
        feasible_mask = (gpu_rem_mem >= s) & (gpu_rem_pressure >= p)
        if not feasible_mask.any():
            continue  # Skip if no feasible GPU
            
        # Score: minimize remaining memory × remaining pressure
        rem_mem = gpu_rem_mem - s
        rem_pressure = gpu_rem_pressure - p
        scores = rem_mem * rem_pressure
        scores[~feasible_mask] = float('inf')
        
        best_gpu = torch.argmin(scores).item()
        
        # Assign model
        assignment_indices[best_gpu].append(idx.item())
        gpu_rem_mem[best_gpu] -= s
        gpu_rem_pressure[best_gpu] -= p
    
    # --- Post-Optimization: Simulated Annealing ---
    def refine_assignment(indices_map: List[List[int]]) -> List[List[int]]:
        def calculate_metrics(gpu_indices):
            w = sum(weights[i].item() for i in gpu_indices)
            s = sum(sizes[i].item() for i in gpu_indices)
            return w, s
        
        def get_kvpr(w, s):
            if s >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                return float('inf')
            return w / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - s)
        
        # Initial state
        current_metrics = [calculate_metrics(gpu_indices) for gpu_indices in indices_map]
        current_kvs = [get_kvpr(w, s) for w, s in current_metrics]
        current_max = max(current_kvs)
        
        best_indices_map = [list(gpu) for gpu in indices_map]
        best_max = current_max
        
        # Annealing parameters
        temperature = 0.02
        cooling_rate = 0.995
        min_temp = 1e-7
        max_iter = 2000
        
        for iteration in range(max_iter):
            if temperature < min_temp:
                break
            
            # Select two non-empty GPUs
            valid_gpus = [i for i in range(gpu_num) if len(indices_map[i]) > 0]
            if len(valid_gpus) < 2:
                break
                
            g1, g2 = random.sample(valid_gpus, 2)
            list1 = indices_map[g1]
            list2 = indices_map[g2]
            
            # Pick random models to swap
            i = random.choice(list1)
            j = random.choice(list2)
            
            # Check memory after swap
            s1_new = sum(sizes[x].item() for x in list1) - sizes[i].item() + sizes[j].item()
            s2_new = sum(sizes[x].item() for x in list2) - sizes[j].item() + sizes[i].item()
            
            if s1_new > GPU_MEM_SIZE or s2_new > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                continue
            
            # Compute new workloads
            w1_old, s1_old = current_metrics[g1]
            w2_old, s2_old = current_metrics[g2]
            
            w1_new = w1_old - weights[i].item() + weights[j].item()
            w2_new = w2_old - weights[j].item() + weights[i].item()
            
            # Compute new KVPRs
            kv1_new = get_kvpr(w1_new, s1_new)
            kv2_new = get_kvpr(w2_new, s2_new)
            
            # Max KVPR after swap
            other_max = max(current_kvs[g] for g in range(gpu_num) if g != g1 and g != g2)
            new_max = max(kv1_new, kv2_new, other_max)
            
            delta = new_max - current_max
            
            # Acceptance probability
            if delta < 0 or random.random() < math.exp(-delta / temperature):
                # Apply swap
                list1.remove(i)
                list1.append(j)
                list2.remove(j)
                list2.append(i)
                
                # Update metrics
                current_metrics[g1] = (w1_new, s1_new)
                current_metrics[g2] = (w2_new, s2_new)
                current_kvs[g1] = kv1_new
                current_kvs[g2] = kv2_new
                current_max = new_max
                
                # Update best solution
                if new_max < best_max:
                    best_max = new_max
                    best_indices_map = [list(gpu) for gpu in indices_map]
            
            # Cool down
            temperature *= cooling_rate
            
            # Adaptive cooling: slow down if stuck
            if iteration > 200 and iteration % 100 == 0 and current_max == best_max:
                temperature *= 0.9
        
        return best_indices_map
    
    refined_indices = refine_assignment(assignment_indices)
    
    # --- Convert to Output Format ---
    result = {}
    for g in range(gpu_num):
        models_on_gpu = [models[i] for i in refined_indices[g]]
        # Sort models by name for deterministic output
        models_on_gpu.sort(key=lambda m: m.model_name)
        result[g] = models_on_gpu
    
    return result

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple
import math
import random
import torch

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    if not models:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    if gpu_num == 1:
        return {0: models}
    
    # Validate constraints
    for m in models:
        if m.model_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"Model {m.model_name} size {m.model_size}GB exceeds GPU memory {GPU_MEM_SIZE}GB")
        if m.slo <= 0:
            raise ValueError(f"Model {m.model_name} has non-positive SLO")
        if m.model_size <= 0:
            raise ValueError(f"Model {m.model_name} has non-positive size")
    
    # Precompute metrics
    n = len(models)
    weights = torch.tensor([m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    sizes = torch.tensor([m.model_size for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # --- Lower Bound Calculation ---
    total_w = weights.sum().item()
    total_s = sizes.sum().item()
    
    # Lower bound based on average utilization
    avg_free = max(0.05, GPU_MEM_SIZE - (total_s / gpu_num))
    lb1 = total_w / gpu_num / avg_free
    
    # Lower bound based on max weight
    lb2 = weights.max().item() / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sizes.max().item())
    lower_bound = max(lb1, lb2)
    
    # --- Binary Search for Optimal KVPR ---
    def is_feasible(limit_kvpr: float) -> bool:
        limit_pressure = limit_kvpr * GPU_MEM_SIZE
        pressures = weights + limit_kvpr * sizes
        
        # Sort by pressure descending
        sorted_indices = torch.argsort(pressures, descending=True)
        
        gpu_rem_mem = torch.full((gpu_num,), GPU_MEM_SIZE, dtype=torch.float32)
        gpu_rem_pressure = torch.full((gpu_num,), limit_pressure, dtype=torch.float32)
        
        for idx in sorted_indices:
            s = sizes[idx].item()
            p = pressures[idx].item()
            
            # Find feasible GPUs
            feasible_mask = (gpu_rem_mem >= s) & (gpu_rem_pressure >= p)
            if not feasible_mask.any():
                return False
            
            # Score: prioritize GPUs with higher free memory and lower pressure utilization
            rem_mem = gpu_rem_mem - s
            rem_pressure = gpu_rem_pressure - p
            
            # Enhanced scoring: balance memory and pressure
            # Use inverse of remaining pressure to favor GPUs with more slack
            scores = rem_mem * (rem_pressure + 1e-6)
            
            # Add bias toward underutilized GPUs
            pressure_util = (limit_pressure - gpu_rem_pressure) / (limit_pressure + 1e-6)
            scores += 0.1 * (1 - pressure_util)
            scores += 0.2 * (gpu_rem_mem - s)
            
            # Random tiebreaker for diversity
            scores += torch.randn(gpu_num) * 1e-4
            
            scores[~feasible_mask] = float('inf')
            
            best_gpu = torch.argmin(scores).item()
            
            gpu_rem_mem[best_gpu] -= s
            gpu_rem_pressure[best_gpu] -= p
            
        return True

    # Binary Search
    low = lower_bound
    high = total_w / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - total_s) if total_s < GPU_MEM_SIZE else 1000.0
    
    # Adaptive upper bound
    utilization_ratio = total_s / (gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE)
    if utilization_ratio > 0.9:
        high *= 1.03
    elif utilization_ratio > 0.8:
        high *= 1.08
    else:
        high *= 1.15
    
    # Run binary search
    for _ in range(60):
        mid = (low + high) / 2
        if is_feasible(mid):
            high = mid
        else:
            low = mid
    
    # --- Final Assignment Construction ---
    final_limit = high
    limit_pressure = final_limit * GPU_MEM_SIZE
    pressures = weights + final_limit * sizes
    
    # Sort by pressure descending
    sorted_indices = torch.argsort(pressures, descending=True)
    
    # Initialize GPU resources
    gpu_rem_mem = torch.full((gpu_num,), GPU_MEM_SIZE, dtype=torch.float32)
    gpu_rem_pressure = torch.full((gpu_num,), limit_pressure, dtype=torch.float32)
    assignment_indices = [[] for _ in range(gpu_num)]
    
    # Assign models greedily
    for idx in sorted_indices:
        s = sizes[idx].item()
        p = pressures[idx].item()
        
        # Check feasible GPUs
        feasible_mask = (gpu_rem_mem >= s) & (gpu_rem_pressure >= p)
        if not feasible_mask.any():
            continue
            
        # Score: prioritize memory-rich GPUs and balanced pressure
        rem_mem = gpu_rem_mem - s
        rem_pressure = gpu_rem_pressure - p
        scores = rem_mem * (rem_pressure + 1e-6)
        
        # Add bias toward GPUs with lower pressure utilization and more free memory
        pressure_util = (limit_pressure - gpu_rem_pressure) / (limit_pressure + 1e-6)
        scores += 0.1 * (1 - pressure_util)
        scores += 0.2 * (gpu_rem_mem - s)
        
        # Add random tiebreaker
        scores += torch.randn(gpu_num) * 1e-4
        
        scores[~feasible_mask] = float('inf')
        
        best_gpu = torch.argmin(scores).item()
        
        # Assign model
        assignment_indices[best_gpu].append(idx.item())
        gpu_rem_mem[best_gpu] -= s
        gpu_rem_pressure[best_gpu] -= p
    
    # --- Post-Optimization: Enhanced Simulated Annealing with Local Search ---
    def refine_assignment(indices_map: List[List[int]]) -> List[List[int]]:
        def calculate_metrics(gpu_indices):
            w = sum(weights[i].item() for i in gpu_indices)
            s = sum(sizes[i].item() for i in gpu_indices)
            return w, s
        
        def get_kvpr(w, s):
            if s >= GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-5:
                return float('inf')
            return w / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - s)
        
        # Initial state
        current_metrics = [calculate_metrics(gpu_indices) for gpu_indices in indices_map]
        current_kvs = [get_kvpr(w, s) for w, s in current_metrics]
        current_max = max(current_kvs)
        
        best_indices_map = [list(gpu) for gpu in indices_map]
        best_max = current_max
        
        # Annealing parameters
        temperature = 0.04
        cooling_rate = 0.992
        min_temp = 1e-8
        max_iter = 3500
        
        for iteration in range(max_iter):
            if temperature < min_temp:
                break
            
            # Select two non-empty GPUs
            valid_gpus = [i for i in range(gpu_num) if len(indices_map[i]) > 0]
            if len(valid_gpus) < 2:
                break
                
            g1, g2 = random.sample(valid_gpus, 2)
            list1 = indices_map[g1]
            list2 = indices_map[g2]
            
            # Pick random models to swap
            i = random.choice(list1)
            j = random.choice(list2)
            
            # Check memory after swap
            s1_new = sum(sizes[x].item() for x in list1) - sizes[i].item() + sizes[j].item()
            s2_new = sum(sizes[x].item() for x in list2) - sizes[j].item() + sizes[i].item()
            
            if s1_new > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-5 or s2_new > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-5:
                continue
            
            # Compute new workloads
            w1_old, s1_old = current_metrics[g1]
            w2_old, s2_old = current_metrics[g2]
            
            w1_new = w1_old - weights[i].item() + weights[j].item()
            w2_new = w2_old - weights[j].item() + weights[i].item()
            
            # Compute new KVPRs
            kv1_new = get_kvpr(w1_new, s1_new)
            kv2_new = get_kvpr(w2_new, s2_new)
            
            # Max KVPR after swap
            other_max = max(current_kvs[g] for g in range(gpu_num) if g != g1 and g != g2)
            new_max = max(kv1_new, kv2_new, other_max)
            
            delta = new_max - current_max
            
            # Acceptance probability with adaptive threshold
            if delta < 0 or random.random() < math.exp(-delta / (temperature * (1 + 0.3 * iteration / max_iter))):
                # Apply swap
                list1.remove(i)
                list1.append(j)
                list2.remove(j)
                list2.append(i)
                
                # Update metrics
                current_metrics[g1] = (w1_new, s1_new)
                current_metrics[g2] = (w2_new, s2_new)
                current_kvs[g1] = kv1_new
                current_kvs[g2] = kv2_new
                current_max = new_max
                
                # Update best solution
                if new_max < best_max:
                    best_max = new_max
                    best_indices_map = [list(gpu) for gpu in indices_map]
            
            # Cool down
            temperature *= cooling_rate
            
            # Adaptive cooling: slower decay if improvement stagnates
            if iteration > 400 and iteration % 200 == 0 and current_max == best_max:
                temperature *= 0.96
                # Slight boost in temperature if stuck to escape local minima
                if temperature < 0.015:
                    temperature = 0.015
        
        return best_indices_map
    
    refined_indices = refine_assignment(assignment_indices)
    
    # --- Convert to Output Format ---
    result = {}
    for g in range(gpu_num):
        models_on_gpu = [models[i] for i in refined_indices[g]]
        # Sort models by name for deterministic output
        models_on_gpu.sort(key=lambda m: m.model_name)
        result[g] = models_on_gpu
    
    # Final validation: ensure all models are placed and constraints met
    total_models = sum(len(models_on_gpu) for models_on_gpu in result.values())
    if total_models != len(models):
        raise RuntimeError("Model count mismatch in final assignment")
    
    for g in range(gpu_num):
        total_size = sum(m.model_size for m in result[g])
        if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise RuntimeError(f"GPU {g} exceeds memory limit: {total_size}GB > {GPU_MEM_SIZE}GB")
    
    return result

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    for m in models:

            raise ValueError(f"Model {m.model_name} size {m.model_size}GB exceeds GPU memory {GPU_MEM_SIZE}GB")

    avg_free = max(0.1, GPU_MEM_SIZE - (total_s / gpu_num))
    lb1 = total_w / gpu_num / avg_free
    
    # Use 95th percentile for stronger outlier handling
    sorted_weights = torch.sort(weights, descending=True).values
    percentile_idx = int(0.95 * n)
    lb2 = sorted_weights[percentile_idx].item() / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sizes[sorted_weights.argmax()].item())
    lower_bound = max(lb1, lb2)
    
    # --- Binary Search for Optimal KVPR ---
    def is_feasible(limit_kvpr: float) -> bool:

            scores += 0.08 * (1 - pressure_util)
            scores += 0.18 * (gpu_rem_mem - s)  # Slightly reduced memory preference
            
            # Add small random jitter to break ties
            scores += torch.randn(gpu_num) * 1e-4
            
            scores[~feasible_mask] = float('inf')
            
            best_gpu = torch.argmin(scores).item()
            
            gpu_rem_mem[best_gpu] -= s
            gpu_rem_pressure[best_gpu] -= p

    # Binary Search
    low = lower_bound
    high = total_w / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - total_s) if total_s < GPU_MEM_SIZE else 1000.0

        high *= 1.06
    else:
        high *= 1.10
    
    # Run binary search with sufficient precision
    for _ in range(60):
        mid = (low + high) / 2
        if is_feasible(mid):
            high = mid
        else:
            low = mid
    
    # --- Final Assignment Construction ---
    final_limit = high
    limit_pressure = final_limit * GPU_MEM_SIZE
    pressures = weights + final_limit * sizes
    
    # Sort by pressure descending
    sorted_indices = torch.argsort(pressures, descending=True)
    
    # Initialize GPU resources
    gpu_rem_mem = torch.full((gpu_num,), GPU_MEM_SIZE, dtype=torch.float32)
    gpu_rem_pressure = torch.full((gpu_num,), limit_pressure, dtype=torch.float32)
    assignment_indices = [[] for _ in range(gpu_num)]
    
    # Assign models greedily with enhanced best-fit heuristic
    for idx in sorted_indices:
        s = sizes[idx].item()
        p = pressures[idx].item()
        
        # Check feasible GPUs
        feasible_mask = (gpu_rem_mem >= s) & (gpu_rem_pressure >= p)
        if not feasible_mask.any():
            continue  # Skip if no feasible GPU
            
        # Score: prioritize memory-rich GPUs and balanced pressure
        rem_mem = gpu_rem_mem - s
        rem_pressure = gpu_rem_pressure - p
        scores = rem_mem * (rem_pressure + 1e-6)
        
        # Add bias toward GPUs with lower pressure utilization and more free memory
        pressure_util = (limit_pressure - gpu_rem_pressure) / (limit_pressure + 1e-6)
        scores += 0.08 * (1 - pressure_util)  # Reduced bias
        scores += 0.18 * (gpu_rem_mem - s)    # Reduced memory preference
        
        # Add random tiebreaker
        scores += torch.randn(gpu_num) * 1e-4
        
        scores[~feasible_mask] = float('inf')
        
        best_gpu = torch.argmin(scores).item()
        
        # Assign model
        assignment_indices[best_gpu].append(idx.item())
        gpu_rem_mem[best_gpu] -= s
        gpu_rem_pressure[best_gpu] -= p
    
    # --- Post-Optimization: Enhanced Simulated Annealing with Local Search ---
    def refine_assignment(indices_map: List[List[int]]) -> List[List[int]]:
        def calculate_metrics(gpu_indices):
            w = sum(weights[i].item() for i in gpu_indices)
            s = sum(sizes[i].item() for i in gpu_indices)
            return w, s
        
        def get_kvpr(w, s):
            if s >= GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-5:
                return float('inf')
            return w / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - s)
        
        # Initial state
        current_metrics = [calculate_metrics(gpu_indices) for gpu_indices in indices_map]
        current_kvs = [get_kvpr(w, s) for w, s in current_metrics]
        current_max = max(current_kvs)
        
        best_indices_map = [list(gpu) for gpu in indices_map]
        best_max = current_max
        
        # Annealing parameters: increased exploration, adaptive cooling
        temperature = 0.05
        cooling_rate = 0.993
        min_temp = 1e-9
        max_iter = 4000
        
        for iteration in range(max_iter):
            if temperature < min_temp:
                break
            
            # Select two non-empty GPUs
            valid_gpus = [i for i in range(gpu_num) if len(indices_map[i]) > 0]
            if len(valid_gpus) < 2:
                break
                
            g1, g2 = random.sample(valid_gpus, 2)
            list1 = indices_map[g1]
            list2 = indices_map[g2]
            
            # Pick random models to swap
            i = random.choice(list1)
            j = random.choice(list2)
            
            # Check memory after swap
            s1_new = sum(sizes[x].item() for x in list1) - sizes[i].item() + sizes[j].item()
            s2_new = sum(sizes[x].item() for x in list2) - sizes[j].item() + sizes[i].item()
            
            if s1_new > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-5 or s2_new > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-5:
                continue
            
            # Compute new workloads
            w1_old, s1_old = current_metrics[g1]
            w2_old, s2_old = current_metrics[g2]
            
            w1_new = w1_old - weights[i].item() + weights[j].item()
            w2_new = w2_old - weights[j].item() + weights[i].item()
            
            # Compute new KVPRs
            kv1_new = get_kvpr(w1_new, s1_new)
            kv2_new = get_kvpr(w2_new, s2_new)
            
            # Max KVPR after swap
            other_max = max(current_kvs[g] for g in range(gpu_num) if g != g1 and g != g2)
            new_max = max(kv1_new, kv2_new, other_max)
            
            delta = new_max - current_max
            
            # Acceptance probability with adaptive threshold
            if delta < 0 or random.random() < math.exp(-delta / (temperature * (1 + 0.2 * iteration / max_iter))):
                # Apply swap
                list1.remove(i)
                list1.append(j)
                list2.remove(j)
                list2.append(i)
                
                # Update metrics
                current_metrics[g1] = (w1_new, s1_new)
                current_metrics[g2] = (w2_new, s2_new)
                current_kvs[g1] = kv1_new
                current_kvs[g2] = kv2_new
                current_max = new_max
                
                # Update best solution
                if new_max < best_max:
                    best_max = new_max
                    best_indices_map = [list(gpu) for gpu in indices_map]
            
            # Cool down
            temperature *= cooling_rate
            
            # Adaptive cooling: slower decay if improvement stagnates
            if iteration > 500 and iteration % 250 == 0 and current_max == best_max:
                temperature *= 0.95
                # Slight boost in temperature if stuck to escape local minima
                if temperature < 0.01:
                    temperature = 0.015
        
        return best_indices_map
    
    refined_indices = refine_assignment(assignment_indices)
    
    # --- Convert to Output Format ---
    result = {}
    for g in range(gpu_num):
        models_on_gpu = [models[i] for i in refined_indices[g]]
        # Sort models by name for deterministic output
        models_on_gpu.sort(key=lambda m: m.model_name)
        result[g] = models_on_gpu
    
    # Final validation: ensure all models are placed and constraints met
    total_models = sum(len(models_on_gpu) for models_on_gpu in result.values())
    if total_models != len(models):
        raise RuntimeError("Model count mismatch in final assignment")
    
    for g in range(gpu_num):
        total_size = sum(m.model_size for m in result[g])
        if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise RuntimeError(f"GPU {g} exceeds memory limit: {total_size}GB > {GPU_MEM_SIZE}GB")
    
    return result

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple
import torch
import random
import math

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    if not models:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    if gpu_num == 1:
        return {0: models}
    
    # Validate constraints
    for m in models:
        if m.model_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"Model {m.model_name} size {m.model_size}GB exceeds GPU memory {GPU_MEM_SIZE}GB")
    
    # Precompute metrics
    n = len(models)
    weights = torch.tensor([m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models], dtype=torch.float64)
    sizes = torch.tensor([m.model_size for m in models], dtype=torch.float64)
    
    # --- Lower Bound Calculation ---
    total_w = weights.sum().item()
    total_s = sizes.sum().item()
    
    # Adjusted average free memory: more conservative lower bound
    avg_free = max(0.1, GPU_MEM_SIZE - (total_s / gpu_num))
    lb1 = total_w / gpu_num / avg_free
    
    # Use 85th percentile to reduce sensitivity to extreme outliers
    sorted_weights = torch.sort(weights, descending=True).values
    percentile_idx = int(0.85 * n)
    lb2 = sorted_weights[percentile_idx].item() / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sizes[sorted_weights.argmax()].item())
    lower_bound = max(lb1, lb2)
    
    # --- Binary Search for Optimal KVPR ---
    def is_feasible(limit_kvpr: float) -> bool:
        limit_pressure = limit_kvpr * GPU_MEM_SIZE
        pressures = weights + limit_kvpr * sizes
        
        # Sort by pressure descending
        sorted_indices = torch.argsort(pressures, descending=True)
        
        gpu_rem_mem = torch.full((gpu_num,), GPU_MEM_SIZE, dtype=torch.float64)
        gpu_rem_pressure = torch.full((gpu_num,), limit_pressure, dtype=torch.float64)
        
        for idx in sorted_indices:
            s = sizes[idx].item()
            p = pressures[idx].item()
            
            # Find feasible GPUs
            feasible_mask = (gpu_rem_mem >= s) & (gpu_rem_pressure >= p)
            if not feasible_mask.any():
                return False
            
            # Score: prioritize GPUs with higher free memory and lower pressure utilization
            rem_mem = gpu_rem_mem - s
            rem_pressure = gpu_rem_pressure - p
            scores = rem_mem * (rem_pressure + 1e-6)
            
            # Enhanced bias: stronger emphasis on balanced pressure utilization
            pressure_util = (limit_pressure - gpu_rem_pressure) / (limit_pressure + 1e-6)
            # More aggressive bias toward underutilized pressure slots
            scores += 0.15 * (1 - pressure_util)  # Increased weight
            scores += 0.15 * (gpu_rem_mem - s)    # Slightly reduced memory preference
            
            # Add small random jitter to break ties and improve diversity
            scores += torch.randn(gpu_num) * 1e-5
            
            scores[~feasible_mask] = float('inf')
            
            best_gpu = torch.argmin(scores).item()
            
            gpu_rem_mem[best_gpu] -= s
            gpu_rem_pressure[best_gpu] -= p
            
        return True

    # Binary Search
    low = lower_bound
    high = total_w / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - total_s) if total_s < GPU_MEM_SIZE else 1000.0
    
    # Tighter high bound with adaptive scaling based on total utilization
    utilization_ratio = total_s / (gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE)
    if utilization_ratio > 0.9:
        high *= 1.02
    elif utilization_ratio > 0.8:
        high *= 1.05
    else:
        high *= 1.10
    
    # Run binary search with sufficient precision
    for _ in range(60):
        mid = (low + high) / 2
        if is_feasible(mid):
            high = mid
        else:
            low = mid
    
    # --- Final Assignment Construction ---
    final_limit = high
    limit_pressure = final_limit * GPU_MEM_SIZE
    pressures = weights + final_limit * sizes
    
    # Sort by pressure descending
    sorted_indices = torch.argsort(pressures, descending=True)
    
    # Initialize GPU resources
    gpu_rem_mem = torch.full((gpu_num,), GPU_MEM_SIZE, dtype=torch.float64)
    gpu_rem_pressure = torch.full((gpu_num,), limit_pressure, dtype=torch.float64)
    assignment_indices = [[] for _ in range(gpu_num)]
    
    # Assign models greedily with enhanced best-fit heuristic
    for idx in sorted_indices:
        s = sizes[idx].item()
        p = pressures[idx].item()
        
        # Check feasible GPUs
        feasible_mask = (gpu_rem_mem >= s) & (gpu_rem_pressure >= p)
        if not feasible_mask.any():
            continue  # Skip if no feasible GPU
            
        # Score: prioritize memory-rich GPUs and balanced pressure
        rem_mem = gpu_rem_mem - s
        rem_pressure = gpu_rem_pressure - p
        scores = rem_mem * (rem_pressure + 1e-6)
        
        # Add bias toward GPUs with lower pressure utilization and more free memory
        pressure_util = (limit_pressure - gpu_rem_pressure) / (limit_pressure + 1e-6)
        scores += 0.15 * (1 - pressure_util)  # Increased bias
        scores += 0.15 * (gpu_rem_mem - s)    # Reduced memory preference
        
        # Add random tiebreaker
        scores += torch.randn(gpu_num) * 1e-5
        
        scores[~feasible_mask] = float('inf')
        
        best_gpu = torch.argmin(scores).item()
        
        # Assign model
        assignment_indices[best_gpu].append(idx.item())
        gpu_rem_mem[best_gpu] -= s
        gpu_rem_pressure[best_gpu] -= p
    
    # --- Post-Optimization: Enhanced Simulated Annealing with Local Search ---
    def refine_assignment(indices_map: List[List[int]]) -> List[List[int]]:
        def calculate_metrics(gpu_indices):
            w = sum(weights[i].item() for i in gpu_indices)
            s = sum(sizes[i].item() for i in gpu_indices)
            return w, s
        
        def get_kvpr(w, s):
            if s >= GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-5:
                return float('inf')
            return w / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - s)
        
        # Initial state
        current_metrics = [calculate_metrics(gpu_indices) for gpu_indices in indices_map]
        current_kvs = [get_kvpr(w, s) for w, s in current_metrics]
        current_max = max(current_kvs)
        
        best_indices_map = [list(gpu) for gpu in indices_map]
        best_max = current_max
        
        # Annealing parameters: increased exploration, adaptive cooling
        temperature = 0.05
        cooling_rate = 0.990
        min_temp = 1e-9
        max_iter = 4000
        
        for iteration in range(max_iter):
            if temperature < min_temp:
                break
            
            # Select two non-empty GPUs
            valid_gpus = [i for i in range(gpu_num) if len(indices_map[i]) > 0]
            if len(valid_gpus) < 2:
                break
                
            g1, g2 = random.sample(valid_gpus, 2)
            list1 = indices_map[g1]
            list2 = indices_map[g2]
            
            # Pick random models to swap
            i = random.choice(list1)
            j = random.choice(list2)
            
            # Check memory after swap
            s1_new = sum(sizes[x].item() for x in list1) - sizes[i].item() + sizes[j].item()
            s2_new = sum(sizes[x].item() for x in list2) - sizes[j].item() + sizes[i].item()
            
            if s1_new > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-5 or s2_new > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-5:
                continue
            
            # Compute new workloads
            w1_old, s1_old = current_metrics[g1]
            w2_old, s2_old = current_metrics[g2]
            
            w1_new = w1_old - weights[i].item() + weights[j].item()
            w2_new = w2_old - weights[j].item() + weights[i].item()
            
            # Compute new KVPRs
            kv1_new = get_kvpr(w1_new, s1_new)
            kv2_new = get_kvpr(w2_new, s2_new)
            
            # Max KVPR after swap
            other_max = max(current_kvs[g] for g in range(gpu_num) if g != g1 and g != g2)
            new_max = max(kv1_new, kv2_new, other_max)
            
            delta = new_max - current_max
            
            # Acceptance probability with adaptive threshold
            if delta < 0 or random.random() < math.exp(-delta / (temperature * (1 + 0.4 * iteration / max_iter))):
                # Apply swap
                list1.remove(i)
                list1.append(j)
                list2.remove(j)
                list2.append(i)
                
                # Update metrics
                current_metrics[g1] = (w1_new, s1_new)
                current_metrics[g2] = (w2_new, s2_new)
                current_kvs[g1] = kv1_new
                current_kvs[g2] = kv2_new
                current_max = new_max
                
                # Update best solution
                if new_max < best_max:
                    best_max = new_max
                    best_indices_map = [list(gpu) for gpu in indices_map]
            
            # Cool down
            temperature *= cooling_rate
            
            # Adaptive cooling: slower decay if improvement stagnates
            if iteration > 500 and iteration % 250 == 0 and current_max == best_max:
                temperature *= 0.97
                # Slight boost in temperature if stuck to escape local minima
                if temperature < 0.01:
                    temperature = 0.01
        
        return best_indices_map
    
    refined_indices = refine_assignment(assignment_indices)
    
    # --- Convert to Output Format ---
    result = {}
    for g in range(gpu_num):
        models_on_gpu = [models[i] for i in refined_indices[g]]
        # Sort models by name for deterministic output
        models_on_gpu.sort(key=lambda m: m.model_name)
        result[g] = models_on_gpu
    
    # Final validation: ensure all models are placed and constraints met
    total_models = sum(len(models_on_gpu) for models_on_gpu in result.values())
    if total_models != len(models):
        raise RuntimeError("Model count mismatch in final assignment")
    
    for g in range(gpu_num):
        total_size = sum(m.model_size for m in result[g])
        if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise RuntimeError(f"GPU {g} exceeds memory limit: {total_size}GB > {GPU_MEM_SIZE}GB")
    
    return result

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple
import torch
import math

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    if not models:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    if gpu_num == 1:
        return {0: models}
    
    # Validate constraints
    for m in models:
        if m.model_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"Model {m.model_name} size {m.model_size}GB exceeds GPU memory {GPU_MEM_SIZE}GB")
    
    # Precompute metrics
    n = len(models)
    weights = torch.tensor([m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models], dtype=torch.float64)
    sizes = torch.tensor([m.model_size for m in models], dtype=torch.float64)
    
    # --- Lower Bound Calculation ---
    total_w = weights.sum().item()
    total_s = sizes.sum().item()
    
    # Lower bound 1: Average load on average free memory
    avg_free = GPU_MEM_SIZE - (total_s / gpu_num)
    if avg_free <= 1e-6:
        lb1 = float('inf')
    else:
        lb1 = (total_w / gpu_num) / avg_free
    
    # Lower bound 2: Individual model pressure (bottleneck)
    lb2 = (weights / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sizes)).max().item()
    
    lower_bound = max(lb1, lb2)
    
    # --- Binary Search for Optimal KVPR ---
    def is_feasible(limit_kvpr: float) -> bool:
        """
        Check feasibility using a greedy heuristic with priority queue.
        Priority is based on 'tightness' - how much pressure a model adds
        relative to the remaining budget.
        """
        limit_pressure = limit_kvpr * GPU_MEM_SIZE
        # Pressure = weight + limit_kvpr * size
        pressures = weights + limit_kvpr * sizes
        
        # Sort by pressure descending (largest first)
        sorted_indices = torch.argsort(pressures, descending=True)
        
        # Initialize GPU states
        gpu_rem_mem = torch.full((gpu_num,), GPU_MEM_SIZE, dtype=torch.float64)
        gpu_rem_pressure = torch.full((gpu_num,), limit_pressure, dtype=torch.float64)
        
        for idx in sorted_indices:
            s = sizes[idx].item()
            p = pressures[idx].item()
            
            # Find GPUs that can fit this model
            feasible_mask = (gpu_rem_mem >= s) & (gpu_rem_pressure >= p)
            if not feasible_mask.any():
                return False
            
            # Best-Fit Strategy:
            # Use a modified score: prioritize GPUs that are already tight on memory or pressure
            # But add a small bias toward underutilized GPUs to avoid overfilling
            rem_mem = gpu_rem_mem - s
            rem_pressure = gpu_rem_pressure - p
            
            # Avoid division by zero or negative values
            rem_mem = torch.clamp(rem_mem, min=0.0)
            rem_pressure = torch.clamp(rem_pressure, min=0.0)
            
            # Score: (rem_mem / GPU_MEM_SIZE) * (rem_pressure / limit_pressure) + 0.01 * (1 - rem_mem / GPU_MEM_SIZE)
            # The extra term encourages spreading to less filled GPUs
            scores = (rem_mem / GPU_MEM_SIZE) * (rem_pressure / limit_pressure)
            scores += 0.01 * (1.0 - rem_mem / GPU_MEM_SIZE)
            scores[~feasible_mask] = float('inf')
            
            best_gpu = torch.argmin(scores).item()
            
            gpu_rem_mem[best_gpu] -= s
            gpu_rem_pressure[best_gpu] -= p
            
        return True

    # Binary Search Execution
    low = lower_bound
    # Upper bound: Worst case single GPU
    if total_s >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
        high = 1000.0
    else:
        high = total_w / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - total_s)
    
    # Add margin to high
    high *= 1.05  # Reduced margin for tighter convergence
    
    # Precision: 50 iterations to speed up
    for _ in range(50):
        mid = (low + high) / 2
        if is_feasible(mid):
            high = mid
        else:
            low = mid
    
    # --- Final Assignment Construction ---
    final_limit = high
    limit_pressure = final_limit * GPU_MEM_SIZE
    pressures = weights + final_limit * sizes
    
    # Sort by pressure descending
    sorted_indices = torch.argsort(pressures, descending=True)
    
    # Initialize GPU resources
    gpu_rem_mem = torch.full((gpu_num,), GPU_MEM_SIZE, dtype=torch.float64)
    gpu_rem_pressure = torch.full((gpu_num,), limit_pressure, dtype=torch.float64)
    assignment_indices = [[] for _ in range(gpu_num)]
    
    # Assign models
    for idx in sorted_indices:
        s = sizes[idx].item()
        p = pressures[idx].item()
        
        # Find feasible GPUs
        feasible_mask = (gpu_rem_mem >= s) & (gpu_rem_pressure >= p)
        if not feasible_mask.any():
            # Handle gracefully: use first GPU with space
            best_gpu = -1
            for g in range(gpu_num):
                if gpu_rem_mem[g] >= s:
                    best_gpu = g
                    break
            if best_gpu == -1:
                raise RuntimeError("Infeasible assignment detected")
        else:
            # Score with stronger preference for balanced load
            rem_mem = gpu_rem_mem - s
            rem_pressure = gpu_rem_pressure - p
            rem_mem = torch.clamp(rem_mem, min=0.0)
            rem_pressure = torch.clamp(rem_pressure, min=0.0)
            
            # Use a more balanced score: emphasize load balance over tightness
            scores = (rem_mem / GPU_MEM_SIZE) * (rem_pressure / limit_pressure)
            scores += 0.005 * (1.0 - rem_mem / GPU_MEM_SIZE)
            scores[~feasible_mask] = float('inf')
            
            best_gpu = torch.argmin(scores).item()
        
        assignment_indices[best_gpu].append(idx.item())
        gpu_rem_mem[best_gpu] -= s
        gpu_rem_pressure[best_gpu] -= p
    
    # --- Post-Optimization: Local Search (Hill Climbing) with Adaptive Threshold ---
    # Enhanced local search with adaptive threshold and model swapping
    # Focus on high-impact swaps with early termination

    def calculate_gpu_metrics(indices_list: List[int]) -> Tuple[float, float]:
        w = sum(weights[i].item() for i in indices_list)
        s = sum(sizes[i].item() for i in indices_list)
        return w, s
    
    def get_kvpr(w: float, s: float) -> float:
        if s >= GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-9:
            return float('inf')
        return w / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - s)
    
    # Current state
    current_map = [list(g) for g in assignment_indices]
    current_metrics = [calculate_gpu_metrics(g) for g in current_map]
    current_kvs = [get_kvpr(w, s) for w, s in current_metrics]
    current_max = max(current_kvs)
    
    best_map = [list(g) for g in current_map]
    best_max = current_max
    
    # Iterative improvement with adaptive threshold
    max_iter = 800
    improvement_found = True
    iteration = 0
    threshold = 1e-8  # Tighter threshold for convergence
    
    while improvement_found and iteration < max_iter:
        improvement_found = False
        iteration += 1
        
        # Identify the bottleneck GPU
        bottleneck_gpu = max(range(gpu_num), key=lambda g: current_kvs[g])
        if current_kvs[bottleneck_gpu] <= 0:
            break
            
        # Try to move a model FROM bottleneck_gpu TO another GPU
        if not current_map[bottleneck_gpu]:
            continue
            
        # Sort models on bottleneck by impact (weight/size ratio), but with randomness for diversity
        # Use random shuffle for top 3 to avoid local minima
        bottleneck_models = sorted(current_map[bottleneck_gpu], 
                                   key=lambda i: weights[i].item() / sizes[i].item(), 
                                   reverse=True)
        
        # Shuffle top 3 to introduce diversity
        top_n = min(3, len(bottleneck_models))
        if top_n > 1:
            import random
            random.shuffle(bottleneck_models[:top_n])
        
        for i in bottleneck_models:
            w_i = weights[i].item()
            s_i = sizes[i].item()
            
            # Candidate destination GPUs (sorted by current KVPR, lowest first)
            dest_gpus = sorted([g for g in range(gpu_num) if g != bottleneck_gpu], 
                               key=lambda g: current_kvs[g])
            
            for g in dest_gpus:
                # Check memory constraint
                if current_metrics[g][1] + s_i > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                
                # Calculate new KVPRs
                w_b_new = current_metrics[bottleneck_gpu][0] - w_i
                s_b_new = current_metrics[bottleneck_gpu][1] - s_i
                w_g_new = current_metrics[g][0] + w_i
                s_g_new = current_metrics[g][1] + s_i
                
                kv_b_new = get_kvpr(w_b_new, s_b_new)
                kv_g_new = get_kvpr(w_g_new, s_g_new)
                
                # Calculate new max KVPR
                other_max = max(current_kvs[h] for h in range(gpu_num) if h != bottleneck_gpu and h != g)
                new_max = max(kv_b_new, kv_g_new, other_max)
                
                # Accept if it improves the max KVPR significantly
                if new_max < current_max - threshold:
                    # Apply move
                    current_map[bottleneck_gpu].remove(i)
                    current_map[g].append(i)
                    
                    # Update metrics
                    current_metrics[bottleneck_gpu] = (w_b_new, s_b_new)
                    current_metrics[g] = (w_g_new, s_g_new)
                    current_kvs[bottleneck_gpu] = kv_b_new
                    current_kvs[g] = kv_g_new
                    current_max = new_max
                    
                    improvement_found = True
                    
                    # Update best solution
                    if new_max < best_max:
                        best_map = [list(m) for m in current_map]
                        best_max = new_max
                    
                    break  # Found a move for this model, try next model
            
            if improvement_found:
                break  # Restart the loop with updated state
    
    # --- Convert to Output Format ---
    result = {}
    for g in range(gpu_num):
        models_on_gpu = [models[i] for i in best_map[g]]
        # Sort by model_name for deterministic output
        models_on_gpu.sort(key=lambda m: m.model_name)
        result[g] = models_on_gpu
    
    return result

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple
import torch
import math

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    if not models:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    if gpu_num == 1:
        return {0: models}
    
    # Validate constraints
    for m in models:
        if m.model_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"Model {m.model_name} size {m.model_size}GB exceeds GPU memory {GPU_MEM_SIZE}GB")
    
    # Precompute metrics
    n = len(models)
    weights = torch.tensor([m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models], dtype=torch.float64)
    sizes = torch.tensor([m.model_size for m in models], dtype=torch.float64)
    
    # --- Lower Bound Calculation ---
    total_w = weights.sum().item()
    total_s = sizes.sum().item()
    
    # Lower bound 1: Average load on average free memory
    avg_free = GPU_MEM_SIZE - (total_s / gpu_num)
    if avg_free <= 1e-6:
        lb1 = float('inf')
    else:
        lb1 = (total_w / gpu_num) / avg_free
    
    # Lower bound 2: Individual model pressure (bottleneck)
    lb2 = (weights / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sizes)).max().item()
    
    lower_bound = max(lb1, lb2)
    
    # --- Binary Search for Optimal KVPR ---
    def is_feasible(limit_kvpr: float) -> bool:
        """
        Check feasibility using a greedy heuristic with priority queue.
        Priority is based on 'tightness' - how much pressure a model adds
        relative to the remaining budget.
        """
        limit_pressure = limit_kvpr * GPU_MEM_SIZE
        # Pressure = weight + limit_kvpr * size
        pressures = weights + limit_kvpr * sizes
        
        # Sort by pressure descending (largest first)
        sorted_indices = torch.argsort(pressures, descending=True)
        
        # Initialize GPU states
        gpu_rem_mem = torch.full((gpu_num,), GPU_MEM_SIZE, dtype=torch.float64)
        gpu_rem_pressure = torch.full((gpu_num,), limit_pressure, dtype=torch.float64)
        
        for idx in sorted_indices:
            s = sizes[idx].item()
            p = pressures[idx].item()
            
            # Find GPUs that can fit this model
            feasible_mask = (gpu_rem_mem >= s) & (gpu_rem_pressure >= p)
            if not feasible_mask.any():
                return False
            
            # Best-Fit Strategy:
            # We want to fill GPUs 'just enough' to balance the load.
            # Score = (rem_mem / GPU_MEM_SIZE) * (rem_pressure / limit_pressure)
            # We want to minimize this score to use tightest slots first.
            rem_mem = gpu_rem_mem - s
            rem_pressure = gpu_rem_pressure - p
            
            # Avoid division by zero or negative values
            rem_mem = torch.clamp(rem_mem, min=0.0)
            rem_pressure = torch.clamp(rem_pressure, min=0.0)
            
            # Score: prioritize GPUs that are already tight on memory or pressure
            # Lower score is better (means we are filling up a tight slot)
            scores = (rem_mem / GPU_MEM_SIZE) * (rem_pressure / limit_pressure)
            scores[~feasible_mask] = float('inf')
            
            best_gpu = torch.argmin(scores).item()
            
            gpu_rem_mem[best_gpu] -= s
            gpu_rem_pressure[best_gpu] -= p
            
        return True

    # Binary Search Execution
    low = lower_bound
    # Upper bound: Worst case single GPU
    if total_s >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
        high = 1000.0
    else:
        high = total_w / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - total_s)
    
    # Add margin to high
    high *= 1.1
    
    # Precision: 60 iterations usually sufficient
    for _ in range(60):
        mid = (low + high) / 2
        if is_feasible(mid):
            high = mid
        else:
            low = mid
    
    # --- Final Assignment Construction ---
    final_limit = high
    limit_pressure = final_limit * GPU_MEM_SIZE
    pressures = weights + final_limit * sizes
    
    # Sort by pressure descending
    sorted_indices = torch.argsort(pressures, descending=True)
    
    # Initialize GPU resources
    gpu_rem_mem = torch.full((gpu_num,), GPU_MEM_SIZE, dtype=torch.float64)
    gpu_rem_pressure = torch.full((gpu_num,), limit_pressure, dtype=torch.float64)
    assignment_indices = [[] for _ in range(gpu_num)]
    
    # Assign models
    for idx in sorted_indices:
        s = sizes[idx].item()
        p = pressures[idx].item()
        
        # Find feasible GPUs
        feasible_mask = (gpu_rem_mem >= s) & (gpu_rem_pressure >= p)
        if not feasible_mask.any():
            # This shouldn't happen if binary search was correct, but handle gracefully
            # Force assign to first GPU with space (should be caught by validation)
            for g in range(gpu_num):
                if gpu_rem_mem[g] >= s:
                    best_gpu = g
                    break
            else:
                raise RuntimeError("Infeasible assignment detected")
        else:
            # Best-Fit scoring
            rem_mem = gpu_rem_mem - s
            rem_pressure = gpu_rem_pressure - p
            rem_mem = torch.clamp(rem_mem, min=0.0)
            rem_pressure = torch.clamp(rem_pressure, min=0.0)
            
            scores = (rem_mem / GPU_MEM_SIZE) * (rem_pressure / limit_pressure)
            scores[~feasible_mask] = float('inf')
            
            best_gpu = torch.argmin(scores).item()
        
        assignment_indices[best_gpu].append(idx.item())
        gpu_rem_mem[best_gpu] -= s
        gpu_rem_pressure[best_gpu] -= p
    
    # --- Post-Optimization: Local Search (Hill Climbing) ---
    # The binary search + best-fit provides a good starting point.
    # We refine it by swapping models between GPUs to reduce the max KVPR.
    
    def calculate_gpu_metrics(indices_list: List[int]) -> Tuple[float, float]:
        w = sum(weights[i].item() for i in indices_list)
        s = sum(sizes[i].item() for i in indices_list)
        return w, s
    
    def get_kvpr(w: float, s: float) -> float:
        if s >= GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-9:
            return float('inf')
        return w / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - s)
    
    # Current state
    current_map = [list(g) for g in assignment_indices]
    current_metrics = [calculate_gpu_metrics(g) for g in current_map]
    current_kvs = [get_kvpr(w, s) for w, s in current_metrics]
    current_max = max(current_kvs)
    
    best_map = [list(g) for g in current_map]
    best_max = current_max
    
    # Iterative improvement
    # We focus on swapping models between the GPU with max KVPR and others
    # to balance the load.
    max_iter = 1000
    improvement_found = True
    iteration = 0
    
    while improvement_found and iteration < max_iter:
        improvement_found = False
        iteration += 1
        
        # Identify the bottleneck GPU
        bottleneck_gpu = max(range(gpu_num), key=lambda g: current_kvs[g])
        if current_kvs[bottleneck_gpu] == 0:
            break
            
        # Try to move a model FROM bottleneck_gpu TO another GPU
        if not current_map[bottleneck_gpu]:
            continue
            
        # Sort models on bottleneck by impact (weight/size ratio)
        bottleneck_models = sorted(current_map[bottleneck_gpu], 
                                   key=lambda i: weights[i].item() / sizes[i].item(), 
                                   reverse=True)
        
        for i in bottleneck_models:
            w_i = weights[i].item()
            s_i = sizes[i].item()
            
            # Candidate destination GPUs (sorted by current KVPR, lowest first)
            # We want to move to GPUs with low KVPR that can accommodate it
            dest_gpus = sorted([g for g in range(gpu_num) if g != bottleneck_gpu], 
                               key=lambda g: current_kvs[g])
            
            for g in dest_gpus:
                # Check memory constraint
                if current_metrics[g][1] + s_i > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                
                # Calculate new KVPRs
                w_b_new = current_metrics[bottleneck_gpu][0] - w_i
                s_b_new = current_metrics[bottleneck_gpu][1] - s_i
                w_g_new = current_metrics[g][0] + w_i
                s_g_new = current_metrics[g][1] + s_i
                
                kv_b_new = get_kvpr(w_b_new, s_b_new)
                kv_g_new = get_kvpr(w_g_new, s_g_new)
                
                # Calculate new max KVPR
                other_max = max(current_kvs[h] for h in range(gpu_num) if h != bottleneck_gpu and h != g)
                new_max = max(kv_b_new, kv_g_new, other_max)
                
                # Accept if it improves the max KVPR
                if new_max < current_max - 1e-9:
                    # Apply move
                    current_map[bottleneck_gpu].remove(i)
                    current_map[g].append(i)
                    
                    # Update metrics
                    current_metrics[bottleneck_gpu] = (w_b_new, s_b_new)
                    current_metrics[g] = (w_g_new, s_g_new)
                    current_kvs[bottleneck_gpu] = kv_b_new
                    current_kvs[g] = kv_g_new
                    current_max = new_max
                    
                    improvement_found = True
                    
                    # Update best solution
                    if new_max < best_max:
                        best_map = [list(m) for m in current_map]
                        best_max = new_max
                    
                    break # Found a move for this model, try next model
            
            if improvement_found:
                break # Restart the loop with updated state
    
    # --- Convert to Output Format ---
    result = {}
    for g in range(gpu_num):
        models_on_gpu = [models[i] for i in best_map[g]]
        # Sort by name for deterministic output
        models_on_gpu.sort(key=lambda m: m.model_name)
        result[g] = models_on_gpu
    
    return result

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple
import math

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    # PARADIGM SHIFT: Constraint Programming via Binary Search on KVPR
    # Instead of optimizing placement directly, we treat the problem as:
    # "Can we achieve max KVPR <= L?" and binary search for the minimum L.
    # The feasibility check uses a heuristic based on GPU "pressure" accumulation.
    
    if not models:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    if gpu_num == 1:
        return {0: models}
    
    # Validate constraints
    for m in models:
        if m.model_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"Model {m.model_name} size {m.model_size}GB exceeds GPU memory {GPU_MEM_SIZE}GB")
    
    # Precompute metrics
    n = len(models)
    weights = [m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models]
    sizes = [m.model_size for m in models]
    
    # Sort models by size descending (pack larger items first)
    sorted_indices = sorted(range(n), key=lambda i: sizes[i], reverse=True)
    
    # --- Feasibility Check: "Pressure-Based" Greedy Packing ---
    def is_feasible(limit_kvpr: float) -> Tuple[bool, List[List[int]]]:
        """
        Check if we can pack all models into GPUs such that KVPR <= limit_kvpr.
        
        Constraint for a GPU: sum(weights) / (80 - sum(sizes)) <= limit_kvpr
        => sum(weights) <= limit_kvpr * (80 - sum(sizes))
        => sum(weights) + limit_kvpr * sum(sizes) <= limit_kvpr * 80
        
        Define 'Pressure' P = weight + limit_kvpr * size.
        We need to partition items into bins such that sum(P) <= limit_kvpr * 80
        AND sum(sizes) <= 80.
        
        Strategy:
        1. Calculate P for each model.
        2. Sort models by P descending (high pressure items first).
        3. Use a Best-Fit heuristic that balances:
           - Remaining Capacity (80 - size)
           - Remaining Pressure Budget (limit_kvpr * 80 - P)
        """
        
        limit_pressure = limit_kvpr * GPU_MEM_SIZE
        pressures = [weights[i] + limit_kvpr * sizes[i] for i in range(n)]
        
        # Sort by pressure descending
        indices_by_pressure = sorted(range(n), key=lambda i: pressures[i], reverse=True)
        
        gpu_rem_mem = [GPU_MEM_SIZE] * gpu_num
        gpu_rem_pressure = [limit_pressure] * gpu_num
        assignment = [[] for _ in range(gpu_num)]
        
        # Heuristic: Prioritize GPUs with better overall fit
        # Use a more robust score: minimize unused capacity and pressure
        for idx in indices_by_pressure:
            s = sizes[idx]
            p = pressures[idx]
            
            # Find feasible GPUs
            feasible_gpus = []
            for g in range(gpu_num):
                if p <= gpu_rem_pressure[g] and s <= gpu_rem_mem[g]:
                    # Use a weighted score: prioritize balance between memory and pressure
                    # Avoid extreme imbalance in either dimension
                    mem_ratio = gpu_rem_mem[g] / GPU_MEM_SIZE
                    press_ratio = gpu_rem_pressure[g] / limit_pressure
                    score = 1.0 / (mem_ratio + press_ratio + 1e-9)  # Higher score = better fit
                    feasible_gpus.append((score, g))
            
            if not feasible_gpus:
                return False, []
            
            # Pick best fit
            feasible_gpus.sort(reverse=True)
            best_gpu = feasible_gpus[0][1]
            
            assignment[best_gpu].append(idx)
            gpu_rem_mem[best_gpu] -= s
            gpu_rem_pressure[best_gpu] -= p
            
        return True, assignment

    # --- Binary Search for Optimal KVPR ---
    # Lower bound: 0
    # Upper bound: Worst case single GPU KVPR
    total_w = sum(weights)
    total_s = sum(sizes)
    if total_s >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
        # This should be caught by validation, but just in case
        # In a real scenario, this means impossible, but we try to place anyway
        # by using a very high upper bound.
        upper_bound = 1000.0
    else:
        upper_bound = total_w / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - total_s)
    
    # Add some buffer
    upper_bound *= 1.5
    
    low = 0.0
    high = upper_bound
    best_assignment = None
    
    # 50 iterations for higher precision
    for _ in range(50):
        mid = (low + high) / 2
        feasible, assignment = is_feasible(mid)
        if feasible:
            high = mid
            best_assignment = assignment
        else:
            low = mid
    
    # --- Reconstruct Result ---
    if best_assignment is None:
        # Fallback: Round Robin (should not happen if validation passed)
        rr = {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
        for i, m in enumerate(models):
            rr[i % gpu_num].append(m)
        return rr
    
    # Map indices back to models
    result = {g: [] for g in range(gpu_num)}
    for g in range(gpu_num):
        for idx in best_assignment[g]:
            result[g].append(models[idx])
    
    # Sort by name for deterministic output
    for g in result:
        result[g].sort(key=lambda m: m.model_name)
    
    # Final validation: ensure no GPU exceeds memory limit
    for g in result:
        total_size = sum(m.model_size for m in result[g])
        if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            # Fallback to round-robin if validation fails
            rr = {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
            for i, m in enumerate(models):
                rr[i % gpu_num].append(m)
            return rr
    
    return result

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Dict, List
import torch
import random
import math

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    if not models:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    if gpu_num == 1:
        return {0: models}
    
    # Validate constraints
    for m in models:
        if m.model_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"Model {m.model_name} size {m.model_size}GB exceeds GPU memory {GPU_MEM_SIZE}GB")
    
    # Precompute metrics
    n = len(models)
    weights = torch.tensor([m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    sizes = torch.tensor([m.model_size for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # --- Lower Bound Calculation ---
    total_w = weights.sum().item()
    total_s = sizes.sum().item()
    
    # Minimum free memory per GPU must be positive
    avg_free = GPU_MEM_SIZE - (total_s / gpu_num)
    if avg_free <= 0:
        lb1 = float('inf')
    else:
        lb1 = total_w / gpu_num / avg_free
    
    # Individual model pressure upper bound
    lb2 = max((weights[i].item() / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sizes[i].item())) for i in range(n))
    lower_bound = max(lb1, lb2)
    
    # --- Binary Search for Optimal KVPR ---
    def is_feasible(limit_kvpr: float) -> bool:
        limit_pressure = limit_kvpr * GPU_MEM_SIZE
        pressures = weights + limit_kvpr * sizes
        
        # Sort by pressure descending
        sorted_indices = torch.argsort(pressures, descending=True)
        
        gpu_rem_mem = torch.full((gpu_num,), GPU_MEM_SIZE, dtype=torch.float32)
        gpu_rem_pressure = torch.full((gpu_num,), limit_pressure, dtype=torch.float32)
        
        for idx in sorted_indices:
            s = sizes[idx].item()
            p = pressures[idx].item()
            
            # Find feasible GPUs
            feasible_mask = (gpu_rem_mem >= s) & (gpu_rem_pressure >= p)
            if not feasible_mask.any():
                return False
            
            # Best fit: minimize remaining memory * pressure
            rem_mem = gpu_rem_mem - s
            rem_pressure = gpu_rem_pressure - p
            scores = rem_mem * rem_pressure
            
            # Mask infeasible
            scores[~feasible_mask] = float('inf')
            
            best_gpu = torch.argmin(scores).item()
            
            gpu_rem_mem[best_gpu] -= s
            gpu_rem_pressure[best_gpu] -= p
            
        return True

    # Binary Search
    low = lower_bound
    high = total_w / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - total_s) if total_s < GPU_MEM_SIZE else 1000.0
    
    # Adjust high if total size is very close to total capacity
    if total_s > gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE * 0.9:
        high *= 1.3
    
    # Run binary search with sufficient precision
    for _ in range(60):
        mid = (low + high) / 2
        if is_feasible(mid):
            high = mid
        else:
            low = mid
    
    # --- Final Assignment Construction ---
    final_limit = high
    limit_pressure = final_limit * GPU_MEM_SIZE
    pressures = weights + final_limit * sizes
    
    # Sort by pressure descending
    sorted_indices = torch.argsort(pressures, descending=True)
    
    # Initialize GPU resources
    gpu_rem_mem = torch.full((gpu_num,), GPU_MEM_SIZE, dtype=torch.float32)
    gpu_rem_pressure = torch.full((gpu_num,), limit_pressure, dtype=torch.float32)
    assignment_indices = [[] for _ in range(gpu_num)]
    
    # Assign models greedily with best-fit heuristic
    for idx in sorted_indices:
        s = sizes[idx].item()
        p = pressures[idx].item()
        
        # Check feasible GPUs
        feasible_mask = (gpu_rem_mem >= s) & (gpu_rem_pressure >= p)
        if not feasible_mask.any():
            continue  # Skip if no feasible GPU
            
        # Score: minimize remaining memory × remaining pressure
        rem_mem = gpu_rem_mem - s
        rem_pressure = gpu_rem_pressure - p
        scores = rem_mem * rem_pressure
        scores[~feasible_mask] = float('inf')
        
        best_gpu = torch.argmin(scores).item()
        
        # Assign model
        assignment_indices[best_gpu].append(idx.item())
        gpu_rem_mem[best_gpu] -= s
        gpu_rem_pressure[best_gpu] -= p
    
    # --- Post-Optimization: Simulated Annealing ---
    def refine_assignment(indices_map: List[List[int]]) -> List[List[int]]:
        def calculate_metrics(gpu_indices):
            w = sum(weights[i].item() for i in gpu_indices)
            s = sum(sizes[i].item() for i in gpu_indices)
            return w, s
        
        def get_kvpr(w, s):
            if s >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                return float('inf')
            return w / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - s)
        
        # Initial state
        current_metrics = [calculate_metrics(gpu_indices) for gpu_indices in indices_map]
        current_kvs = [get_kvpr(w, s) for w, s in current_metrics]
        current_max = max(current_kvs)
        
        best_indices_map = [list(gpu) for gpu in indices_map]
        best_max = current_max
        
        # Annealing parameters
        temperature = 0.02
        cooling_rate = 0.995
        min_temp = 1e-7
        max_iter = 2000
        
        for iteration in range(max_iter):
            if temperature < min_temp:
                break
            
            # Select two non-empty GPUs
            valid_gpus = [i for i in range(gpu_num) if len(indices_map[i]) > 0]
            if len(valid_gpus) < 2:
                break
                
            g1, g2 = random.sample(valid_gpus, 2)
            list1 = indices_map[g1]
            list2 = indices_map[g2]
            
            # Pick random models to swap
            i = random.choice(list1)
            j = random.choice(list2)
            
            # Check memory after swap
            s1_new = sum(sizes[x].item() for x in list1) - sizes[i].item() + sizes[j].item()
            s2_new = sum(sizes[x].item() for x in list2) - sizes[j].item() + sizes[i].item()
            
            if s1_new > GPU_MEM_SIZE or s2_new > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                continue
            
            # Compute new workloads
            w1_old, s1_old = current_metrics[g1]
            w2_old, s2_old = current_metrics[g2]
            
            w1_new = w1_old - weights[i].item() + weights[j].item()
            w2_new = w2_old - weights[j].item() + weights[i].item()
            
            # Compute new KVPRs
            kv1_new = get_kvpr(w1_new, s1_new)
            kv2_new = get_kvpr(w2_new, s2_new)
            
            # Max KVPR after swap
            other_max = max(current_kvs[g] for g in range(gpu_num) if g != g1 and g != g2)
            new_max = max(kv1_new, kv2_new, other_max)
            
            delta = new_max - current_max
            
            # Acceptance probability
            if delta < 0 or random.random() < math.exp(-delta / temperature):
                # Apply swap
                list1.remove(i)
                list1.append(j)
                list2.remove(j)
                list2.append(i)
                
                # Update metrics
                current_metrics[g1] = (w1_new, s1_new)
                current_metrics[g2] = (w2_new, s2_new)
                current_kvs[g1] = kv1_new
                current_kvs[g2] = kv2_new
                current_max = new_max
                
                # Update best solution
                if new_max < best_max:
                    best_max = new_max
                    best_indices_map = [list(gpu) for gpu in indices_map]
            
            # Cool down
            temperature *= cooling_rate
            
            # Adaptive cooling: slow down if stuck
            if iteration > 200 and iteration % 100 == 0 and current_max == best_max:
                temperature *= 0.9
        
        return best_indices_map
    
    refined_indices = refine_assignment(assignment_indices)
    
    # --- Convert to Output Format ---
    result = {}
    for g in range(gpu_num):
        models_on_gpu = [models[i] for i in refined_indices[g]]
        # Sort models by name for deterministic output
        models_on_gpu.sort(key=lambda m: m.model_name)
        result[g] = models_on_gpu
    
    return result

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple
import torch
import random
import math

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    if not models:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    if gpu_num == 1:
        return {0: models}
    
    # Validate constraints
    for m in models:
        if m.model_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"Model {m.model_name} size {m.model_size}GB exceeds GPU memory {GPU_MEM_SIZE}GB")
    
    # Precompute metrics
    n = len(models)
    weights = torch.tensor([m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    sizes = torch.tensor([m.model_size for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # --- Lower Bound Calculation ---
    total_w = weights.sum().item()
    total_s = sizes.sum().item()
    
    # Adjusted average free memory: penalize high utilization with stronger penalty
    avg_free = max(0.05, GPU_MEM_SIZE - (total_s / gpu_num))
    lb1 = total_w / gpu_num / avg_free
    
    # Use 90th percentile for robustness against outliers
    sorted_weights = torch.sort(weights, descending=True).values
    percentile_idx = int(0.90 * n)
    lb2 = sorted_weights[percentile_idx].item() / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sizes[sorted_weights.argmax()].item())
    lower_bound = max(lb1, lb2)
    
    # --- Binary Search for Optimal KVPR ---
    def is_feasible(limit_kvpr: float) -> bool:
        limit_pressure = limit_kvpr * GPU_MEM_SIZE
        pressures = weights + limit_kvpr * sizes
        
        # Sort by pressure descending
        sorted_indices = torch.argsort(pressures, descending=True)
        
        gpu_rem_mem = torch.full((gpu_num,), GPU_MEM_SIZE, dtype=torch.float32)
        gpu_rem_pressure = torch.full((gpu_num,), limit_pressure, dtype=torch.float32)
        
        for idx in sorted_indices:
            s = sizes[idx].item()
            p = pressures[idx].item()
            
            # Find feasible GPUs
            feasible_mask = (gpu_rem_mem >= s) & (gpu_rem_pressure >= p)
            if not feasible_mask.any():
                return False
            
            # Score: prioritize GPUs with higher free memory and lower pressure utilization
            rem_mem = gpu_rem_mem - s
            rem_pressure = gpu_rem_pressure - p
            scores = rem_mem * (rem_pressure + 1e-6)
            
            # Enhanced bias: favor GPUs with balanced load (both memory and pressure)
            pressure_util = (limit_pressure - gpu_rem_pressure) / (limit_pressure + 1e-6)
            # More aggressive bias toward underutilized pressure slots
            scores += 0.1 * (1 - pressure_util)  # Increased weight
            scores += 0.2 * (gpu_rem_mem - s)    # Stronger memory preference
            
            # Add small random jitter to break ties and improve diversity
            scores += torch.randn(gpu_num) * 1e-4
            
            scores[~feasible_mask] = float('inf')
            
            best_gpu = torch.argmin(scores).item()
            
            gpu_rem_mem[best_gpu] -= s
            gpu_rem_pressure[best_gpu] -= p
            
        return True

    # Binary Search
    low = lower_bound
    high = total_w / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - total_s) if total_s < GPU_MEM_SIZE else 1000.0
    
    # Tighter high bound with adaptive scaling based on total utilization
    utilization_ratio = total_s / (gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE)
    if utilization_ratio > 0.9:
